metadata_source: Local apt-cache binary and source metadata from the Ubuntu 24.04 repositories configured on this system.
selection_policy: Representative direct dependents on libexif12 and/or source packages that Build-Depend on libexif-dev. This list is intentionally diverse rather than exhaustive.
notes: Runtime-functionality summaries are based on package descriptions when those descriptions are explicit, and otherwise on conservative inference from the package's role plus its direct libexif dependency.
A compile-time-only entry is included when the Ubuntu source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev but the main packaged runtime binaries do not directly depend on libexif12.

dependents.name: exif
dependents.runtime_functionality: Shows EXIF information embedded in JPEG files from the command line.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows exif Depends on libexif12 and the exif source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly says exif is a command-line utility to show EXIF information hidden in JPEG files.

dependents.name: exiftran
dependents.runtime_functionality: Performs lossless JPEG rotation using the EXIF orientation tag and updates EXIF dimensions, orientation, and thumbnails.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows exiftran Depends on libexif12 and the fbi source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly says exiftran rotates images automatically by checking the EXIF orientation tag and updates EXIF information and thumbnails.

dependents.name: eog-plugin-exif-display
dependents.runtime_functionality: Displays EXIF-derived camera data and a histogram inside GNOME Image Viewer.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows eog-plugin-exif-display Depends on libexif12 and the eog-plugins source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly says the plugin displays a histogram and other camera data from EXIF.

dependents.name: eog-plugin-map
dependents.runtime_functionality: Shows the image's location on a map using EXIF GPS metadata.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows eog-plugin-map Depends on libexif12 and the eog-plugins source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly says the plugin provides a map to show the image's location data.

dependents.name: tracker-extract
dependents.runtime_functionality: Extracts EXIF metadata from image files during desktop indexing so photo metadata becomes searchable.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows tracker-extract Depends on libexif12 and the tracker-miners source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.

The package description identifies tracker-extract as the metadata-extractor component of Tracker; the EXIF-specific role is a conservative inference from that role plus its direct libexif dependency.

dependents.name: Shotwell
dependents.runtime_functionality: Imports and manages photos using EXIF-backed metadata such as capture properties and orientation information.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows shotwell Depends on libexif12 and the shotwell source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.

The package description identifies Shotwell as a digital photo organizer that imports images from disk or camera and supports operations such as rotation and tagging; the EXIF-specific role is a conservative inference from that photo-management role plus its direct libexif dependency.

dependents.name: FoxtrotGPS
dependents.runtime_functionality: Reads photo metadata for the application's geocoding-photos feature.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows foxtrotgps Depends on libexif12 and the foxtrotgps source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly lists geocoding photos as a supported feature.

dependents.name: gphoto2
dependents.runtime_functionality: Works with digital camera images from the command line, including EXIF-aware handling of downloaded camera photos.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows gphoto2 Depends on libexif12 and the gphoto2 source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description identifies gphoto2 as a digital camera command-line client; the EXIF-specific role is a conservative inference from that role plus its direct libexif dependency.

dependents.name: GTKam
dependents.runtime_functionality: Transfers photos and movies from digital cameras and uses libexif-backed GTK widgets for camera-photo EXIF display or editing in the GUI.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows gtkam Depends on libexif12 and libexif-gtk5, and the gtkam source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev and libexif-gtk-dev.
The package description identifies GTKam as a GTK application for retrieving media from digital cameras.

dependents.name: MiniDLNA
dependents.runtime_functionality: Indexes shared picture collections for DLNA clients using JPEG EXIF metadata during photo scanning.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows minidlna Depends on libexif12 and the minidlna source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description says MiniDLNA serves pictures to DLNA clients; the EXIF-specific indexing role is a conservative inference from that role plus its direct libexif dependency.

dependents.name: Gerbera
dependents.runtime_functionality: Processes photo media for UPnP/DLNA serving, using EXIF metadata when indexing JPEG images.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows gerbera Depends on libexif12 and the gerbera source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description says Gerbera streams digital media over UPnP; the EXIF-specific photo-metadata role is a conservative inference from that role plus its direct libexif dependency.

dependents.name: ruby-exif
dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses, edits, and saves EXIF data from Ruby code.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows ruby-exif Depends on libexif12 and the ruby-exif source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly says ruby-exif is a Ruby library for parsing, editing, and saving EXIF data.

dependents.name: libexif-gtk3
dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides GTK 3 widgets that applications use to display and edit EXIF tags.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows libexif-gtk3-5 Depends on libexif12 and the libexif-gtk source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
The package description explicitly says this library provides GTK+ 3.0 widgets to display and edit EXIF tags.

dependents.name: CamlImages
dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides OCaml image-processing code with EXIF-capable image handling in the library runtime.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata shows libcamlimages-ocaml Depends on libexif12 and the camlimages source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev.

The package description identifies CamlImages as an OCaml image-processing library that loads and saves multiple image formats; the EXIF-specific role is a conservative inference from that role plus its direct libexif dependency.

dependents.name: ImageMagick
dependents.notes: Ubuntu's imagemagick source package Build-Depends on libexif-dev, but the main noble runtime libraries and binaries do not directly depend on libexif12 in package metadata.
evidence: Ubuntu source metadata shows imagemagick Build-Depends on libexif-dev.
Ubuntu binary package metadata for imagemagick and libmagickcore-6.q16-7t64 does not list a direct dependency on libexif12, so this is recorded as a compile-time dependency only.
